briefdescription.para: types of counted events
arrays and elements are different gl calls
types of gl primitives counters
if true only last frame of force redraw after events will be swapped, otherwise each will be swapped
minimum auto increment force redraw frames after events
Member variables ///.

detaileddescription.para: A function to reset setting to initial state Overrides should call previous function

briefdescription.para: Stores all the viewing options.
List of registered plugins.
pos to save
size to save
if true - calls render without system events
max fps if animating

detaileddescription.para: this parameter can force up/down mouse scroll useful for WebAssembler version because it has too powerful scroll

briefdescription.para: opengl-based pick window radius in pixels

detaileddescription.para: init in resetSettingsFunction()

briefdescription.para: Experimental/developer features enabled.

detaileddescription.para: init in resetSettingsFunction()

briefdescription.para: command arguments, each parsed arg should be erased from here not to affect other parsers
Stores clipping plane mesh.
class that updates viewer title
the minimum number of frames to be rendered even if the scene is unchanged
Should be <= forceRedrawFrames_. The next N frames will not be shown on screen.
special plugin for menu (initialized before splash window starts)
this flag is needed to know if all viewer setup was already done, and we can call draw
Accumulate launch params from cmd args.
provides non const access to viewer
provide const access to viewer
Launch viewer with given params.
Starts event loop.
Terminate window.
get full parameters with witch viewer was launched

detaileddescription.para: Mesh IO Check the supported file format
Load objects / scenes from files Note! load files with progress bar in next frame if it possible, otherwise load directly inside this function

briefdescription.para: Save first selected objects to file.
Callbacks.
Touch callbacks (now used in EMSCRIPTEN build only)
Touchpad gesture callbacks.
This function is called when window should close, if return value is true, window will stay open.
Draw everything.
Draw 3d scene with UI.

detaileddescription.para: Draw 3d scene without UI framebuffer - bound framebuffer (needed if depth peeling is enabled)

briefdescription.para: Call this function to force redraw scene into scene texture.
Setup viewports views.
Cleans framebuffers for all viewports (sets its background)
OpenGL context resize.
explicitly set framebuffer size
external resize due to user interaction
external set position due to user interaction
external set maximized due to user interaction
external set iconified due to user interaction
external focus handler due to user interaction
external rescale due to user interaction
called when close signal received

detaileddescription.para: Multi-mesh methods /// reset objectRoot with newRoot, append all RenderObjects and basis objects

briefdescription.para: removes all objects from scene
Multi-viewport methods ///.

detaileddescription.para: Return the current viewport, or the viewport corresponding to a given unique identifier
Inputs: viewportId unique identifier corresponding to the desired viewport (current viewport if 0)
Append a new "slot" for a viewport (i.e., copy properties of the current viewport, only changing the viewport size/position)
Inputs: viewport Vector specifying the viewport origin and size in screen coordinates. append_empty If true, existing meshes are hidden on the new viewport.
Returns the unique id of the newly inserted viewport. There can be a maximum of 31 viewports created in the same viewport. Erasing a viewport does not change the id of other existing viewports
Calculates and returns viewports bounds in gl space: (0,0) - lower left angle
Erase a viewport
Inputs: index index of the viewport to erase
Retrieve viewport index from its unique identifier Returns -1 if not found
Get unique id of the vieport containing the mouse if mouse is out of any viewport returns index of last selected viewport

briefdescription.para: Same, but returns an invalid ID if no viewport is hovered, instead of returning the last selected viewport.
Change selected_core_index to the viewport containing the mouse.

detaileddescription.para: Calls fitData for single/each viewport in viewer fill = 0.6 parameter means that scene will 0.6 of screen, snapView - to snap camera angle to closest canonical quaternion

memberdef.argsstring: (const Box3f &box, MR::ViewportMask vpList=MR::ViewportMask::all(), float fill=0.6f, bool snapView=true)
memberdef.name: fitBoxViewport

detaileddescription.para: Calls fitBox for single/each viewport in viewer fill = 0.6 parameter means that scene will 0.6 of screen, snapView - to snap camera angle to closest canonical quaternion
Calls fitData and change FOV to match the screen size then params - params fit data

briefdescription.para: Returns memory amount used by shared GL memory buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Increment number of forced frames to redraw in event loop if swapOnLastOnly only last forced frame will be present on screen and all previous will not

briefdescription.para: Forces redraw no later than the specified number of frames.
Returns true if current frame will be shown on display.
Returns number of events of given type.
Returns number of events of given type.
Increment number of gl primitives drawed in this frame.
Returns mask of present viewports.
Restes frames counter and events counter.

detaileddescription.para:
Captures 3d scene
resolution
resolution of the image <= 0 means default
transparentBg
don't draw background / viewport borders

memberdef.argsstring: (std::function< void(const Image &)> callback, const Vector2i &pos=Vector2i(), const Vector2i &size=Vector2i())
memberdef.name: captureUIScreenShot

detaileddescription.para:
Captures part of window in the beginning of next frame, capturing all that was drawn in this frame
callback
will be called right when screenshot is taken
pos
left-bottom corner of capturing area relative of left-down corner of window. default = size(0, 0)
size
size of capturing area. default = size(0, 0) - auto size to right-top corner of window.

briefdescription.para: Returns true if can enable alpha sort.

detaileddescription.para: Tries to enable alpha sort, returns true if value was changed, return false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Returns true if alpha sort is enabled, false otherwise.
Returns number of passes performed by depth peeler.
Sets desired number of passes to depth peeler.
Returns true if depth peeling is enabled, false otherwise.
Returns if scene texture is now bound.

detaileddescription.para: Binds or unbinds scene texture (should be called only with valid window) note that it does not clear framebuffer

briefdescription.para: Returns true if 3d scene is rendering in scene texture instead of main framebuffer.

detaileddescription.para: Returns actual msaa level of: scene texture if it is present, or main framebuffer
Requests changing MSAA level if scene texture is using, request should be executed in the beginig of next frame otherwise restart of the app is required to apply change to main framebuffer

briefdescription.para: Returns MSAA level that have been requested (might be different from actual MSAA using, because of GPU limitations or need to restart app)

detaileddescription.para: Sets manager of viewer settings which loads user personal settings on beginning of app and saves it in app's ending

briefdescription.para: Finds point in all spaces from screen space pixel point.
Finds point under mouse in all spaces and under mouse viewport id.

detaileddescription.para: Converts screen space coordinate to viewport space coordinate (0,0) if viewport does not exist screen space: X [0,framebufferSize.x], Y [0,framebufferSize.y] - (0,0) is upper left of window viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0,1] - 0 is Dnear, 1 is Dfar

Converts viewport space coordinate to screen space coordinate (0,0) if viewport does not exist screen space: X [0,framebufferSize.x], Y [0,framebufferSize.y] - (0,0) is upper left of window viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0,1] - 0 is Dnear, 1 is Dfar

briefdescription.para: Returns viewports satisfying given mask.
Enables or disables global history (clears it on disable)
Return true if global history is enabled, false otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: Appends history action to current stack position (clearing redo) if global history is disabled do nothing
Applies undo if global history is enabled return true if undo was applied
Applies redo if global history is enabled return true if redo was applied
Returns global history store returns nullptr if history store is not available

briefdescription.para: Return spacemouse handler.
This method is called after successful scene saving to update scene root, window title and undo.
Get/Set menu plugin (which is separated from other plugins to be inited first before splash window starts)
get menu plugin casted in RibbonMenu
Get the menu plugin casted in given type.
sets stop event loop flag (this flag is glfwShouldWindowClose equivalent)
get stop event loop flag (this flag is glfwShouldWindowClose equivalent)

detaileddescription.para: return true if window should close calls interrupt signal and if no slot interrupts return true, otherwise return false
returns true if viewer has valid GL context note that sometimes it is not enough, for example to free GL memory in destructor, glInitialized_ can be already reset and it requires loadGL() check too

briefdescription.para: update the title of the main window and, if any scene was opened, show its filename
returns true if the system framebuffer is scaled (valid for macOS and Wayland)
return the structure with all viewer's signals

detaileddescription.para: emplace event at the end of the queue replace last skipable with new skipable

briefdescription.para: pop all events from the queue while they have this name
Store of recently opened files.
returns whether to sort the filenames received from Drag&Drop in lexicographical order before adding them in scene
sets whether to sort the filenames received from Drag&Drop in lexicographical order before adding them in scene
draw 2d (UI) part of objects in scene

detaileddescription.para: return true if ImGui Multi Viewport is available (it is unavailable for macOS, Linux, and Emscripten)

briefdescription.para: Init window.
Called from launchInit_ after window creating to configure it properly.
Return true if OpenGL loaded successfully.
Init base objects.
Init all plugins on start.
Shut all plugins at the end.
returns true if was swapped

memberdef.argsstring: (bool fullscreen, int &width, int &height, const std::string &name, int major, int minor)
memberdef.name: tryCreateWindow_

briefdescription.para: creates glfw window with gl version major.minor, false if failed;
(re)initializes the handler of SpaceMouse events
recalculate pixel ratio

detaileddescription.para: return MSAA that is required for framebuffer sceneTextureOn - true means that app is using scene texture for rendering (false means that scene is rendered directly in main framebuffer) forSceneTexture - true request MSAA required for scene texture (calling with !sceneTextureOn is invalid), false - request MSAA for main framebuffer

briefdescription.para: returns global instance of Viewer class
GLFW-based mesh viewer.
